title: Eschweilera micrantha
taxonomy: Eschweilera micrantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, commonly known as the Brazil nut family. It belongs to the genus Eschweilera, which contains around 100 species of trees and shrubs native to the Americas. The plant is native to tropical regions of Central and South America, including countries such as Costa Rica and Ecuador. Eschweilera micrantha is a relatively small tree, typically growing up to 10 meters in height, with a broad, rounded crown and glossy leaves. The plant's classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.

intro: Eschweilera micrantha, also known as the "Brazilian cherry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eschweilera micrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
